page: Amazon Web Services c6gn.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and custom 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 48 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Network connectivity is a key feature, delivering a baseline bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 72814.16, placing it in the top 25% of tested hardware. Conversely, single-core performance is more modest, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1519.29 and a Geekbench single-core score of 974.0, both falling into average and weak categories respectively. Memory bandwidth is stable, recording 167.25 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 75.13 GB/sec for write operations. The instance demonstrates average capabilities in multi-threaded decompression and software compilation, but shows weaknesses in single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and text processing.

The instance exhibits a clear tradeoff between high multi-core throughput and limited single-threaded execution speed. Large language model inference tasks show average performance for medium 7B models but drop to poor or weak tiers for larger 70B models. Given the high baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps and balanced multi-core capabilities, this instance is suited for network-intensive compute workloads, multi-threaded batch processing, static web serving, and distributed database operations that do not require local SSD storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and powered by 48 dedicated ARM64 physical cores via the AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor. Operating at 2.5 GHz, it includes 96.0 GB of memory, resulting in 2.0 GB per core, and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance features a high baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 72814.16, though single-core performance remains average to weak. Memory read bandwidth reaches 167.25 GB/sec for large blocks. This hardware profile is suited for network-bound compute workloads, multi-threaded batch processing, and static web serving, offering a cost-efficient alternative to x86 architectures for workloads optimized for ARM64 multi-core scaling.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.12xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated Graviton cores, 96 GB RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core performance.

tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring forty-eight dedicated cores and high-speed seventy-five gigabit network bandwidth for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores
Equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory and no local storage
Delivers a high baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps
Strong multi-core performance with average single-core execution speeds
Suited for network-intensive compute, static web serving, and multi-threaded batch jobs
